case_title: EVN INVESTMENTS, LLC ET AL VS. SHULGIN AVIATION, LLC ET AL

docket_entries.description: REPLY IN SUPPORT OF ITS MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT OR COMPEL ARBITRATION (TRANSACTION ID # 66139374) FILED BY DEFENDANT SHULGIN AVIATION, LLC

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501,DEFENDANT SHULGIN AVIATION, LLC MOTION TO DISMISS AND OR COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED AS REQUESTED. THE GRAVAMEN OF THIS CASE IS THE DISTRIBUTION OF FUNDS UNDER THE OPERATING AGREEMENT BY THE SIGNATORIES TO THE AGREEMENT; AN ISSUE THAT IS INDISPUTABLY GOVERNED BY THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE. THIS COMPLAINT, NAMING ONLY THE RECIPIENT OF THE FUNDS, AND NOT THE TRANSFEROR-SIGNATORY TO THE OPERATING AGREEMENT APPEARS TO BE AN ATTEMPT TO AVOID ARBITRATION AND TO SEEK THE COURT'S DETERMINATION ON THE ISSUE OF PROPRIETY OF THE DISTRIBUTION OF AND/OR DIVERSION OF FUNDS. SUCH ATTEMPT, WHILE LEGALLY CREATIVE, UNDERMINES THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE AND WILL NOT BE CONDONED BY THE COURT. WHILE THE COURT WILL NOT COMPEL ARBITRATION BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND SHULGIN AVIATION, THE ACTION WILL BE STAYED UNTIL SUCH TIME A DETERMINATION IS MADE IN ARBITRATION REGARDING ALLEGATIONS IN PARAGRAPH 24 AND 25 OF THE COMPLAINT AS TO PLAINTIFF'S ENTITLEMENT TO THE FUNDS UNDER THE OPERATING AGREEMENT. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE MOVING POINTS AND AUTHORITIES ARE DEVOID OF ANY AUTHORITIES ON THIS POINT AND THERE IS NO SHOWING OF COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 128.5 (CLAIMED TO BE THE BASIS FOR THE REQUEST IN REPLY). CCP 128.5 (F)(1)(A)-(B).PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Motion To Dismiss And Or Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Courtcall:
Alberto J. Campain, Esq., (310) 887-1850, appearing for EVN Investments, LLC and
Vladimir Evseev, Plaintiff
Gary I. Blackman, Esq., (312) 476-7536, appearing for Shulgin Aviation, LLC, Defendant

Hearing is held. All counsel stated that they do not contest the court's tentative ruling.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SHULGIN AVIATION, LLC Motion To Dismiss And Or Compel Arbitration is DENIED as requested. The gravamen of this case is the distribution of funds under the Operating Agreement by the signatories to the agreement; an issue that is indisputably governed by the arbitration clause. This complaint, naming only the recipient of the funds, and not the transferor-signatory to the Operating Agreement appears to be an attempt to avoid arbitration and to seek the Court's determination on the issue of propriety of the distribution of and/or diversion of funds. Such attempt, while legally creative, undermines the arbitration clause and will not be condoned by the Court. While the Court will not compel arbitration between Plaintiff and Shulgin Aviation, the action will be stayed until such time a determination is made in arbitration regarding allegations in paragraph 24 and 25 of the Complaint as to Plaintiff's entitlement to the funds under the Operating Agreement. Request for Sanctions is DENIED. The moving Points and Authorities are devoid of any authorities on this point and there is no showing of compliance with CCP 128.5 (claimed to be the basis for the request in reply). CCP 128.5 (f)(1)(A)-(B).Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Dec-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Courtcall:
Alberto J. Campain, Esq., (310) 887-1850, appearing for EVN Investments, LLC and
Vladimir Evseev, Plaintiff
Gary I. Blackman, Esq., (312) 476-7536, appearing for Shulgin Aviation, LLC, Defendant

Hearing is held. All counsel stated that they do not contest the court's tentative ruling.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SHULGIN AVIATION, LLC MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE is GRANTED. Order for the same signed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
